# Extraction fixtures

Binary fixtures used by the OCR module tests (`src/lib/ocr/*.test.ts`).

All three files are **committed to git** so tests are deterministic and
CI doesn't need to install Tesseract language data or PDF generators to
read them. Only the Tesseract test ever exercises the PNG, and that
test is gated behind `RUN_TESSERACT_TESTS=1`.

## Files

| File                 | Purpose                                                                   |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `text-pdf.pdf`       | A two-line PDF with a real text layer. `PdfTextExtractor` should succeed. |
| `image-only.pdf`     | A PDF whose only content is an embedded PNG. No text layer.               |
| `boarding-image.png` | A PNG with the word "BOARDING" in large black letters on white.           |

## Regenerating

```bash
pnpm tsx tests/fixtures/extraction/build.ts
```

The generator script lives next door as `build.ts`. It uses
[`pdf-lib`](https://pdf-lib.js.org/) for the PDFs and
[`sharp`](https://sharp.pixelplumbing.com/) to rasterise an SVG into
the boarding-pass PNG. Both are devDependencies.

If you add a new fixture:

1. Extend `build.ts` with another `buildXxx()` step.
2. Re-run the script.
3. Commit the regenerated bytes alongside the script change so reviewers
   can verify both.

## Why pre-generate, not regenerate per test run?

- Speed: CI doesn't burn time on `sharp` cold starts or PDF assembly.
- Determinism: reviewers see exactly the bytes the tests see.
- Diff hygiene: an unrelated `pdf-lib` upgrade can change the bytes;
  treating them as build outputs lets us notice and audit that
  intentionally.
